buyer_dependent_items.text: Confirm the S2C scope out of the internal CFO scope meeting
Provide sandbox test scenarios and schedule the implementation deep-dive/workshop

competitive_position.summary: GEP is the single strongest current threat as of Jul 2026: named alongside Zycus as a shortlist-to-two finalist and characterised as a 'strong threat' on 11 May 2026, with no evidence of elimination since. iValua was the other named 'strong threat' in May but has not recurred and has faded. Coupa has weakened, with the buyer stating on 1 Jul 2026 that the S2C scope shift 'Put Zycus in stronger position vs Coupa'. SAP Ariba was in the initial Jan 2026 field but silent since, effectively dormant. Recency-weighted rank: GEP (high, active finalist) > Coupa (low, faded/negative) > iValua (low, faded) > Ariba (dormant).

competitors.name: GEP
competitors.quote: confirmed by email- shortlisted to 2 for further implementation plans, commercials and hands on sandbox- need urgent and solid local reference. GEP, iValua strong threats here.
competitors.how_we_win: Deliver a sharper, de-risked phased S2C plan with the RACI/project plan the buyer said competitors gave and we did not, and lead with Merlin AI (Intake, AP OCR, agentic tail-spend negotiation) plus Australian references (Nissan, TataPlay) as the S2C automation edge.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: likely to now move from S2P to S2C (P2P will come early new year). Put Zycus in stronger position vs Coupa.
competitors.how_we_win: Press the S2C-specific value and de-risk the phased approach (P2P deferred to 2027) so Coupa cannot re-enter if full S2P scope returns.

competitors.name: iValua
competitors.quote: shortlisted to 2 for further implementation plans, commercials and hands on sandbox- need urgent and solid local reference. GEP, iValua strong threats here.
competitors.how_we_win: iValua was a May finalist but has not been re-mentioned; hold the reference and implementation lead and monitor for re-surfacing before acting.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.how_we_win: Named in the initial RFP field but silent since January; no active countermove required.

champion_strength.source: Salesforce Contact Role (Evaluator, Primary); Next Step 30 Jun / 1 Jul 2026; Avoma RFP briefing 12 Jan 2026
champion_strength.summary: Tej Ram is an active operational champion and project lead who drives the RFP and keeps Zycus informed (responded to Carl 29-30 Jun, offered a review meeting 1 Jul), but strength is developing because he has not secured direct CFO engagement for Zycus, has not introduced the US-parent approvers, and did not pre-empt the RACI/project-plan gap the buyer flagged. The move is to have him arrange a direct CFO conversation right after the 13 Jul scope decision.

sources: Avoma attendee lists (no CFO present on any call)
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: The economic buyer is the Melbourne-based CFO, who is confirmed budget authority: he instructed the RFP be initiated ('CFO requested to initiate RFP around 5th January', Next Step 12 Dec 2025) and coordinates final sign-off with the TTI US parent company on costings. He has never appeared on a single Zycus-recorded call or commercial discussion across 16 months, and TTI is meeting him internally the week of 13 Jul 2026 to decide scope. His name is not disclosed in Salesforce, so he is mapped by role and authority but not engaged. The move is to have Tej secure a direct Zycus-to-CFO conversation immediately after the 13 Jul scope decision to validate budget and pricing.

recommended_moves: Send Tej the revised S2C pricing, phased costing, and a proper RACI/project plan before the 13 Jul CFO meeting.
Ask Tej to arrange a direct Zycus-to-CFO conversation immediately after the 13 Jul scope decision.
Lock the hands-on sandbox and an ECC6-to-S/4HANA implementation deep-dive with Duncan.
